what_it_does: Registers global window objects (MS_TOKENS, MS_STATUS, MS_SPACE, MS_RADIUS, MS_ELEVATION, MS_TYPE, MS_MOTION) that map semantic design-token names to CSS custom-property references, with validation, loud error logging, and a magenta sentinel for unknown color tokens.
observations: MS_TOKENS maps semantic names like gold, ink, success to -- CSS variables and returns var(--...) references.
MS_STATUS defines tone palettes and a dictionary of status ids with labels and tones.
MS_SPACE, MS_RADIUS, MS_ELEVATION, MS_TYPE, and MS_MOTION provide validated accessors for spacing, radius, shadow, typography, and motion tokens.
Every accessor logs console.error for unknown keys instead of silently falling back.
MS_TOKENS.value() resolves a token to the live computed value from document.documentElement.
DEFAULT_ACCENT is hardcoded as #E0C010 once inside this file as the canonical brand default seed.
MS_MOTION exposes named choreography curves via _curves for app.jsx to read.
Spacing STEPS contains duplicate pixel values for different step keys (7/8 = 32, 9/10 = 40).
standouts: The explicit no-silent-fallbacks policy with a magenta #ff00ff sentinel for missing color tokens.
The single-source-of-truth intent: JS color references must come through these validated token accessors.
Named motion curves exposed as a stable dictionary for the transition tweak in app.jsx.
conventions: Uses IIFE modules attached to window for namespacing.
CSS custom properties are treated as the source of truth; JS only references them.
Semantic token names are preferred over literal values.
Validation happens at the API surface with console.error for misuse.
Comments explain rationale, mapping to CSS files, and intended usage.
concerns: value() reads getComputedStyle on document.documentElement at call time and may fail if called before the document is ready or outside a browser.
Global window pollution for every token module increases runtime surface area.
Spacing map duplicates pixel values across step keys, which may confuse consumers.
console.error in production could be noisy and is not always actionable for end users.
DEFAULT_ACCENT is a hardcoded hex literal, even though the file's stated policy is to avoid them.
notes: This file is the JS-side gatekeeper for design tokens, forcing color, spacing, type, and motion references from JavaScript through validated CSS custom-property accessors.
questions: Where are the corresponding CSS custom properties defined, and how is sync with these maps enforced?
How does app.jsx consume DEFAULT_ACCENT and _curves, and does it guard document readiness for value() calls?
Are the duplicate spacing step values (7/8 and 9/10) intentional aliases or an oversight?
Is the magenta sentinel acceptable in production UI, or should unknown tokens throw instead?
Are there tests that verify every token in MAP has a matching CSS variable?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to abolish hardcoded hex literals and centralize brand values; the file largely does this by mapping semantic names to CSS vars, though it still hardcodes DEFAULT_ACCENT as a seed and keeps literal px maps for validation.
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent value drift across JS files by making token access explicit, validated, and theme-linked, while also giving status and motion semantics a stable vocabulary.
novelty: The defensive no-silent-fallbacks policy combined with a visible magenta sentinel for missing tokens is a distinctive design choice.
contribution: Provides a validated, semantic bridge between JavaScript and CSS custom properties for colors, status, spacing, radius, elevation, typography, and motion, which a fused system would keep as the canonical JS-side token API.
summary_for_embedding: A JavaScript design-token registry exposing window.MS_TOKENS, MS_STATUS, MS_SPACE, MS_RADIUS, MS_ELEVATION, MS_TYPE, and MS_MOTION to return validated CSS custom-property references for theming.
intention_for_embedding: To centralize and enforce JavaScript access to brand tokens so JS-rendered surfaces stay synchronized with CSS theming and avoid hardcoded values.

symbols.does: Looks up the CSS custom property name for a semantic token and logs an error if unknown.
symbols.description: Validates a token name against the MAP dictionary and returns the corresponding -- CSS variable name, or null when the token is not recognized.

symbols.does: Returns a CSS var() reference for a token.
symbols.description: Resolves a token name via propName and returns 'var(--...)' for inline styles and SVG fill/stroke; returns the magenta sentinel if the token is unknown.

symbols.does: Resolves a token to its computed CSS value.
symbols.description: Looks up the CSS custom property and reads the live computed value from the document root, returning the literal string or the magenta sentinel.

symbols.does: Returns the foreground/background pair for a tone name.
symbols.description: Validates a tone key against TONES and returns the color and background CSS var pair, falling back to default with an error if unknown.

symbols.does: Returns full status metadata for a status id.
symbols.description: Looks up a status id in ENTRIES, merges its label and tone with the corresponding tone color pair, and returns a default object with an error if the id is unknown.

symbols.does: Returns a var(--s-*) reference for a spacing step.
symbols.description: Validates the step against STEPS and returns the CSS custom-property reference, logging an error and returning '0px' for unknown steps.

symbols.does: Returns the pixel value for a spacing step.
symbols.description: Validates the step and returns its integer pixel value from STEPS, or 0 with an error for unknown steps.

symbols.does: Finds the closest on-grid spacing step for a raw pixel value.
symbols.description: Iterates over STEPS and returns the step key whose pixel value has the smallest absolute difference to the input.

symbols.does: Returns a var(--r-*) reference for a radius name.
symbols.description: Validates the radius name against R and returns the CSS custom-property reference, logging an error and returning '0px' for unknown names.

symbols.does: Returns the pixel value for a radius name.
symbols.description: Returns the integer corner radius from R or logs an error and returns 0 for unknown names.

symbols.does: Returns a var(--shadow-*) reference for an elevation name.
symbols.description: Validates the elevation name against E and returns the CSS custom-property reference, logging an error and returning 'none' for unknown names.

symbols.does: Returns a var(--fs-*) font-size reference.
symbols.description: Validates the size name against SIZES and returns the CSS custom-property reference, logging an error and returning '13px' for unknown sizes.

symbols.does: Returns the pixel font size for a size name.
symbols.description: Returns the integer pixel size from SIZES or logs an error and returns 13 for unknown sizes.

symbols.does: Returns a var(--tracking-*) letter-spacing reference.
symbols.description: Validates the tracking name against TRACK and returns the CSS custom-property reference, logging an error and returning '0' for unknown names.

symbols.does: Returns a var(--font-*) font-family reference.
symbols.description: Validates the font name against FONTS and returns the CSS custom-property reference, logging an error and returning 'inherit' for unknown names.

symbols.does: Finds the closest named font size for a raw pixel value.
symbols.description: Iterates over SIZES and returns the size key whose pixel value has the smallest absolute difference to the input.

symbols.does: Returns a var(--dur-*) duration reference.
symbols.description: Validates the duration name against DUR and returns the CSS custom-property reference, logging an error and returning '200ms' for unknown names.

symbols.does: Returns a var(--ease-*) easing reference.
symbols.description: Validates the easing name against EASE and returns the CSS custom-property reference, logging an error and returning 'ease' for unknown names.

symbols.does: Returns a named cubic-bezier choreography curve.
symbols.description: Validates the curve name against CURVES and returns the literal cubic-bezier string, logging an error and defaulting to smooth for unknown names.

symbols.does: Builds a CSS transition shorthand.
symbols.description: Concatenates a property with resolved duration and easing references, defaulting to base duration and out easing.
